name: Privacy-respecting on-device extraction for high-end users
thesis: Local-first extraction in regions with strict consumer-data rules (EU GDPR, Russia 152-FZ) lets the candidate operate where peers stall on cross-border data flow.
experience_sketch: Player processes the match locally on a recent smartphone.
Only the rating delta is uploaded; raw video stays on-device.
Player feels personal data has not left the phone.
Player chooses the candidate over peers that require server upload.
Player promotes the privacy stance inside their club.

defensibility.reasoning: Regulatory gate — the cost of compliance is non-trivial for global peers, and local-first extraction scales without that overhead.

evidence.quant_signal: Russian 152-FZ and EU GDPR set explicit cross-border constraints; detailed by FIP regulatory references on regional events (https://www.padelfip.com/events/fip-silver-kazakhstan-2025/).

risks.counter_mechanic: Apple or Google enables on-device transcoding for video apps as a default; on-device CV becomes a commodity.

kill_experiment.description: Ship an on-device pilot for an iPhone 14 or newer; measure energy and accuracy against the cloud baseline.
kill_experiment.success_threshold: On-device runs at less than 2x energy of cloud baseline with accuracy ≥90 percent.

dependencies.stack_fit: Core ML / TFLite; OpenRouter Haiku for on-device prompt evaluation.
